output_debit_classifier.reason: Target purchased tea, beverages, and refreshments for client entertainment purposes. The transaction explicitly states the business purpose (client entertainment), so the expense is classified as Entertainment Expense. The $1,200 base amount plus $120 sales tax (10% × $1,200) totals $1,320. Payment was made by corporate credit card, creating a liability (Credit Card Payable). The sales tax is recoverable input tax (HST Input Tax Credit in Ontario) since this is a business expense, so it is recorded as a separate debit to track the recoverable amount.
